items.headline: Mixed Sentiment as Geopolitical Tensions Persist; S&P 500 Hits Record
items.summary: Global markets are navigating a complex environment characterized by resilient equities, supported by strong corporate earnings and artificial intelligence optimism, yet overshadowed by ongoing geopolitical triggers and rising oil prices. The U.S. economy demonstrated continued growth in the first quarter of 2026, expanding at a 2% annualized pace, an acceleration from the previous quarter's 0.5% increase, although it fell slightly short of economists' forecasts of 2.3%. The Federal Reserve remains hesitant to cut interest rates amid the ongoing conflict in Iran and oil prices exceeding $100 a gallon. The S&P 500 closed April at a fresh record high, marking a 10.4% monthly gain, its strongest performance since November 2020.

items.statContext: Up 0.3% on May 1st, 2026. Up 10.4% for April. Up 5.6% YTD.
Up 0.9% on May 1st, 2026. Strongest monthly gain since 2020 for April. Up 8.1% YTD.
Down 0.3% on May 1st, 2026. Best monthly performance since November 2024 for April. Up 3% YTD.

items.headline: Apple (AAPL)
items.summary: Shares rose after a strong first-quarter earnings report and a better-than-expected outlook for the current quarter.

items.headline: SanDisk (SNDK)
items.summary: The memory chip supplier saw a significant jump following strong first-quarter earnings.

items.headline: Veeva Systems Inc. (VEEV)
items.summary: The stock surged following its inclusion in the S&P 500 benchmark, strong cloud-software demand, and an upbeat growth outlook.

items.headline: Shuttle Pharmaceuticals Holdings, Inc. (SHPH)
items.summary: Experienced a significant decline, partly attributed to news regarding United Dogecoin going public in a merger.

items.headline: Cohen & Company Inc (COHN)

items.headline: Brazil Potash Corp. (GRO)
items.summary: Declined following the announcement of its $55 million public offering of common shares and pre-funded warrants.

items.headline: U.S. Economy Grows 2% in Q1 2026 Amid Geopolitical Headwinds
items.summary: The U.S. economy expanded at a 2% annualized rate in the first quarter of 2026, marking an acceleration from the previous quarter but falling short of economists' projections. Challenges such as the ongoing conflict involving Iran and elevated oil prices are seen as potential headwinds for future growth.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady, Internal Divisions Emerge
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's FOMC voted 8-4 to leave interest rates unchanged, a decision widely anticipated but notable for the four dissenting votes—the highest number since late 1992, signaling a hawkish stance. Outgoing Fed Chair Jerome Powell confirmed he would remain on the Board of Governors, while Kevin Warsh is slated to become the new Chair. Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari suggested the committee should signal flexibility for either a rate cut or hike, given the economic uncertainty linked to the Iran conflict.

items.headline: Berkshire Hathaway's Q1 Operating Earnings Jump, Cash Pile Nears $400 Billion
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. released its first-quarter 2026 earnings, reporting operating earnings of $11.35 billion, an 18% increase year-over-year. The conglomerate's cash reserves surged to a record high of nearly $400 billion.

items.headline: Meta Platforms Shares Decline on Increased AI Capital Expenditure Outlook
items.summary: Shares of Meta Platforms declined approximately 10% over the past week following its first-quarter 2026 update. The company raised its 2026 capital expenditure outlook to between $125 billion and $145 billion, citing higher component costs, particularly memory pricing, which concerned investors. Despite this, Q1 revenue grew 33% year-over-year to $56.3 billion.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge to $120 Amid Middle East Conflict
items.summary: Oil prices briefly crossed $120 a barrel this week, the first time since 2022, primarily driven by the escalating conflict involving Iran, which has disrupted global energy flows and raised concerns about higher inflation and slower global growth. Brent crude later fell 3.41% to $114.01 per barrel after eight consecutive upward sessions.

items.headline: Tech and Energy Lead, Software and Advertising Lag in Sector Performance
items.summary: Technology and software stocks were among the top performers on May 1st. Year-to-date, semiconductor equipment and materials are up 47.6%, and computer hardware stocks are up 45.3%. The Energy sector has also seen strong performance due to surging oil prices. Conversely, software application stocks are the worst performers, down 22.7% YTD, followed by advertising, down 20.9% YTD, and gambling, down 20.7% YTD.
